description:
The project involves building a web-based offline, on-premise application that
uses AI engines to assist with the transcription and/or translation of audio
files collected during investigations. The application will process audio files
and generate a transcription and/or translation that will then be reviewed and
edited by transcribers/translators prior to disclosure. The goal of the project
is to increase the efficiency of the transcription/translation process, not to
replace it. It will help to reduce backlogs and improve operational necessity
by allowing investigations timelier access to relevant details of their operation
which could impact surveillance, officer safety and potential Authorization re-
writes. Transcribers/translators will still need to review the generated
transcriptions for accuracy and make any necessary edits.
qa_section_text:
Section 3.1: Impact Questions and Answers
Reasons for Automation
1. What is motivating your team to introduce automation into this decision-making process?
(Check all that apply)
Existing backlog of work or cases
Use innovative approaches
2. What client needs will the system address and how will this system meet them? If possible,
describe how client needs have been identified.
Transcribing and translating audio is a labour and time intensive process. The
use of AI engines to assist with the transcription/translation will reduce the
amount of labour and time required by transcribers/translators by generating
a draft transcription that they can then review and edit instead of performing
the transcription/translation themselves from scratch.
3. Please describe any public benefits the system is expected to have.
Increasing the efficiency of the transcription/translation of audio files will
allow investigators timelier access to relevant details of their operation which
could impact surveillance, officer safety and potential Authorization re-writes.
It will also impact how quickly disclosure can be provided to the court,
potentially increasing the rate at which public cases are processed.
4. How effective will the system likely be in meeting client needs?
Moderately effective [ Points: +1 ]
5. Please describe any improvements, benefits, or advantages you expect from using an
automated system. This could include relevant program indicators and performance targets.
The automation of the transcription/translation process will increase the
efficiency of the process by a factor of at least 10. This will allow
investigators more timely access to information important for their
operations, and enable disclosure to the courts to be completed more quickly.
6. Please describe how you will ensure that the system is confined to addressing the client
needs identified above.
The application uses a login based on Active Directory to limit access to only
users with proper authorization. SOPs will be put in place to ensure the
proper use of the system. Testing will be done, and feedback gathered, to
ensure that the efficiency and accuracy of the transcriptions/translations
generated by the application are within an acceptable range.
7. Please describe any trade-offs between client interests and program objectives that you have
considered during the design of the project.
We have considered clients' privacy and security concerns. The system is
designed by the RCMP, operated offline and on-premise. No operational data
is stored by the system. Once the transcriptions have been generated and
downloaded by the user, the original audio and the transcription are deleted
from the system.
8. Have alternative non-automated processes been considered?
Yes [ Points: +0 ]
9. If non-automated processes were considered, why was automation identified as the preferred
option?
Manual transcription/translation requires a significant amount of labour and
time from transcribers/translators and has led to a backlog of audio files
waiting to be transcribed, thus delaying disclosure. This application will
automatically generate the transcriptions/translations and the transcribers/
translators will only have to review them and perform edits which is less
labour and time intensive than transcribing them from scratch.
10. What would be the consequence of not deploying the system?
Other (please specify)
11. Please describe
The transcription process will continue as it is now, at the same efficiency
level
Risk Profile
12. Is the project within an area of intense public scrutiny (e.g. because of privacy concerns)
and/or frequent litigation?
No [ Points: +0 ]
13. Are clients in this line of business particularly vulnerable?
No [ Points: +0 ]
14. Are stakes of the decisions very high?
No [ Points: +0 ]
15. Will this project have major impacts on staff, either in terms of their numbers or their roles?
No [ Points: +0 ]
16. Will the use of the system create or exacerbate barriers for persons with disabilities?
No [ Points: +0 ]
Project Authority
17. Will you require new policy authority for this project?
No [ Points: +0 ]
About the Algorithm
18. The algorithm used will be a (trade) secret
No [ Points: +0 ]
19. The algorithmic process will be difficult to interpret or to explain
No [ Points: +0 ]
About the Decision
20. Please describe the decision(s) that will be automated.
The system does not make any decisions. Transcribers/translators will be
reviewing all transcriptions for accuracy and editing them when necessary.
The raw transcriptions generated by the AI engine will not be relied on without
that review and editing process by a human.
21. Does the decision pertain to any of the categories below (check all that apply):
Other (please specify) [ Points: +1 ]
22. Please describe
The system does not make decisions
Impact Assessment
23. Which of the following best describes the type of automation you are planning?
Partial automation (the system will contribute to

Algorithmic Impact Assessment Results
Version: 0.10.0
Project Details
1.Name of Respondent
Christie Sainsbury
2.Job Title
Manager, SSDU
3.Department
Royal Canadian Mounted Police
4.Branch
Specialized Policing Services
5.Project Title
Voice2Text
6.Project ID from IT Plan
N/A
7.Departmental Program (from Department Results Framework)
Technical Investigation Services
8.Project Phase
Design [ Points: 0 ]
9.Please provide a project description:
The project involves building a web-based offline, on-premise application that
uses AI engines to assist with the transcription and/or translation of audio
files collected during investigations. The application will process audio files
and generate a transcription and/or translation that will then be reviewed and
edited by transcribers/translators prior to disclosure. The goal of the project
is to increase the efficiency of the transcription/translation process, not to
replace it. It will help to reduce backlogs and improve operational necessity
by allowing investigations timelier access to relevant details of their operation
which could impact surveillance, officer safety and potential Authorization re-
writes. Transcribers/translators will still need to review the generated
transcriptions for accuracy and make any necessary edits.
About The System
10.Please check which of the following capabilities apply to your system.
Text and speech analysis: Analyzing large data sets to recognize, process, and
tag text, speech, voice, and make recommendations based on the tagging
Section 1: Impact Level : 1
Current Score: 28
Raw Impact Score: 28
Mitigation Score: 6
Section 2: Requirements Specific to Impact Level 1
Peer review
None
Gender-based Analysis Plus
None
Notice
None
Human-in-the-loop for decisions
Decisions may be rendered without direct human involvement.
Explanation
In addition to any applicable legal requirement, ensure that a meaningful explanation is published
for common decision results. The explanation must provide a general description of:
the role of the system in the decision-making process;
input data, its source and method of collection;
the criteria used to evaluate input data and the operations applied to process it;
the output produced by the system and any relevant information needed to interpret it in the
context of the administrative decision; and
the principal factors behind a decision.
Explanations must also inform clients of relevant recourse options, where appropriate.
Descriptions must be made available in plain language through the Algorithmic Impact
Assessment and discoverable via a departmental website.
Training
None
IT and business continuity management
None
Approval for the system to operate
None
Other requirements
The Directive on Automated Decision-Making also includes other requirements that must be met
for all impact levels.
Link to the Directive on Automated Decision-Making
Contact your institution's ATIP office to discuss the requirement for a Privacy Impact
Assessment as per the Directive on Privacy Impact Assessment.
Section 3: Questions and Answers
Section 3.1: Impact Questions and Answers
Reasons for Automation
1. What is motivating your team to introduce automation into this decision-making process?
(Check all that apply)
Existing backlog of work or cases
Use innovative approaches

describe how client needs have been identified.
Transcribing and translating audio is a labour and time intensive process. The

allow investigators timelier access to relevant details of their operation which
could impact surveillance, officer safety and potential Authorization re-writes.
It will also impact how quickly disclosure can be provided to the court,
potentially increasing the rate at which public cases are processed.
4. How effective will the system likely be in meeting client needs?
Moderately effective [ Points: +1 ]

The automation of the transcription/translation process will increase the
efficiency of the process by a factor of at least 10. This will allow

considered during the design of the project.
We have considered clients' privacy and security concerns. The system is

is stored by the system. Once the transcriptions have been generated and
downloaded by the user, the original audio and the transcription are deleted

Yes [ Points: +0 ]

Manual transcription/translation requires a significant amount of labour and
time from transcribers/translators and has led to a backlog of audio files
waiting to be transcribed, thus delaying disclosure. This application will
automatically generate the transcriptions/translations and the transcribers/
translators will only have to review them and perform edits which is less
labour and time intensive than transcribing them from scratch.

Other (please specify)

The transcription process will continue as it is now, at the same efficiency
level
Risk Profile
12. Is the project within an area of intense public scrutiny (e.g. because of privacy concerns)
and/or frequent litigation?
No [ Points: +0 ]
13. Are clients in this line of business particularly vulnerable?
No [ Points: +0 ]
14. Are stakes of the decisions very high?
No [ Points: +0 ]
15. Will this project have major impacts on staff, either in terms of their numbers or their roles?
No [ Points: +0 ]
16. Will the use of the system create or exacerbate barriers for persons with disabilities?
No [ Points: +0 ]
Project Authority
17. Will you require new policy authority for this project?
No [ Points: +0 ]
About the Algorithm
18. The algorithm used will be a (trade) secret
No [ Points: +0 ]
19. The algorithmic process will be difficult to interpret or to explain
No [ Points: +0 ]
About the Decision
20. Please describe the decision(s) that will be automated.
The system does not make any decisions. Transcribers/translators will be
reviewing all transcriptions for accuracy and editing them when necessary.
The raw transcriptions generated by the AI engine will not be relied on without
that review and editing process by a human.
21. Does the decision pertain to any of the categories below (check all that apply):
Other (please specify) [ Points: +1 ]
22. Please describe
The system does not make decisions
Impact Assessment
23. Which of the following best describes the type of automation you are planning?
Partial automation (the system will contribute to administrative decision-
making by supporting an officer through assessments, recommendations,
intermediate decisions, or other outputs) [ Points: +2 ]
24. Please describe the role of the system in the decision-making process.
The system does not make decisions. Transcriptions generated by the AI
engine will not be relied on as-is for any decision making or for disclosure.
They must be reviewed and edited by a transcriber/translator before being
released.
25. Will the system be making decisions or assessments that require judgement or discretion?
No [ Points: +0 ]
26. Please describe the criteria used to evaluate client data and the operations applied to
process it.
Client data are audio files that are uploaded to the application. They are
processed by the AI speech recognition model to generate a transcription/
translation.
27. Please describe the output produced by the system and any relevant information needed to
interpret it in the context of the administrative decision.
The output of the system is a text file transcription of the input audio file. The
original audio file is needed by verify the accuracy of the text transcription by
a transcriber/translator.
28. Will the system perform an assessment or other operation that would not otherwise be
completed by a human?
No [ Points: +0 ]
29. Is the system used by a different part of the organization than the ones who developed it?
No [ Points: +0 ]
30. Are the impacts resulting from the decision reversible?
Reversible [ Points: +1 ]
31. How long will impacts from the decision last?
Impacts are most likely to be brief [ Points: +1 ]
32. Please describe why the impacts resulting from the decision are as per selected option
above.
Transcriptions are never used in raw format from the application. They must
be reviewed by a human transcriber/translator for accuracy before they are
released.
33. The impacts that the decision will have on the rights or freedoms of individuals will likely be:
Little to no impact [ Points: +1 ]
34. Please describe why the impacts resulting from the decision are as per selected option
above.
No impact on the rights of individuals. Audio uploaded to the system is
lawfully obtained. The application doesn't have to be used if manual
transcription is required/preferred.
35. The impacts that the decision will have on the equality, dignity, privacy, and autonomy of
individuals will likely be:
Little to no impact [ Points: +1 ]
36. Please describe why the impacts resulting from the decision are as per selected option
above.
No impact to any individual. Voice2Text is a tool to assist transcribers at the
RCMP, not replace them.
37. The impacts that the decision will have on the health and well-being of individuals will likely
be:
Little to no impact [ Points: +1 ]
38. Please describe why the impacts resulting from the decision are as per selected option
above.
The system does not store the audio files or transcriptions long-term. Once
the transcriptions are completed and downloaded, all data is deleted from the
system. The application does not collect any personal information of the
user.
39. The impacts that the decision will have on the economic interests of individuals will likely
be:
Little to no impact [ Points: +1 ]
40. Please describe why the impacts resulting from the decision are as per selected option
above.
No economic impact.
41. The impacts that the decision will have on the ongoing sustainability of an environmental
ecosystem, will likely be:
Little to no impact [ Points: +1 ]
42. Please describe why the impacts resulting from the decision are as per selected option
above.
No impact on sustainability or environment.
About the Data - A. Data Source
43. Will the Automated Decision System use personal information as input data?
No [ Points: +0 ]
44. What is the highest security classification of the input data used by the system? (Select one)
Protected B / Protected C [ Points: +3 ]
45. Who controls the data?
Federal government [ Points: +1 ]
46. Will the system use data from multiple different sources?
Yes [ Points: +4 ]
47. Will the system require input data from an Internet- or telephony-connected device? (e.g.
Internet of Things, sensor)
No [ Points: +0 ]
48. Will the system interface with other IT systems?
Yes [ Points: +4 ]
49. Who collected the data used for training the system?
A foreign government or non-government third party [ Points: +4 ]
50. Who collected the input data used by the system?
Your institution [ Points: +1 ]
51. Please describe the input data collected and used by the system, its source, and method of
collection.
Input data used in the system is lawfully collected by the RCMP as evidence
using various means. It is uploaded to the system, transcribed/translated,
and subsequently deleted from the system after the transcription has been
downloaded.
About the Data - B. Type of Data
52. Will the system require the analysis of unstructured data to render a recommendation or a
decision?
No [ Points: 0 ]
Section 3.2: Mitigation Questions and Answers
Consultations
1. Internal Stakeholders (federal institutions, including the federal public service)
Yes [ Points: +1 ]
2. Which Internal Stakeholders will you be engaging?
Data Governance
Legal Services
Communications services
Human Resources
Digital Policy
3. External Stakeholders (groups in other sectors or jurisdictions)
No [ Points: +0 ]
De-Risking and Mitigation Measures - Data Quality
4. Will you have documented processes in place to test datasets against biases and other
unexpected outcomes? This could include experience in applying frameworks, methods,
guidelines or other assessment tools.
No [ Points: +0 ]
5. Will you be developing a process to document how data quality issues were resolved during
the design process?
No [ Points: +0 ]
6. Will you be making this information publicly available?
No [ Points: +0 ]
7. Will you undertake a Gender Based Analysis Plus of the data?
No [ Points: +0 ]
8. Will you be making this information publicly available?
No [ Points: +0 ]
9. Have you assigned accountability in your institution for the design, development,
maintenance, and improvement of the system?
Yes [ Points: +2 ]
10. Will you have a documented process to manage the risk that outdated or unreliable data is
used to make an automated decision?
No [ Points: +0 ]
11. Will you be making this information publicly available?
No [ Points: +0 ]
12. Will the data used for this system be posted on the Open Government Portal?
No [ Points: +0 ]
De-Risking and Mitigation Measures - Procedural
Fairness
13. Will the audit trail identify the authority or delegated authority identified in legislation?
No [ Points: +0 ]
14. Will the system provide an audit trail that records all the recommendations or decisions
made by the system?
No [ Points: +0 ]
15. Will all key decision points be identifiable in the audit trail?
No [ Points: +0 ]
16. Will all key decision points within the automated system's logic be linked to the relevant
legislation, policy or procedure?
No [ Points: +0 ]
17. Will you maintain a log detailing all of the changes made to the model and the system?
No [ Points: +0 ]
18. Will the audit trail clearly set out all decision points made by the system?
No [ Points: +0 ]
19. Could the audit trail generated by the system be used to help generate a notification of the
decision (including a statement of reasons or other notification) where required?
No [ Points: +0 ]
20. Will the audit trail identify precisely which version of the system was used for each decision
it supports?
No [ Points: +0 ]
21. Will the audit trail show who the authorized decision-maker is?
No [ Points: +0 ]
22. Will the system be able to produce reasons for its decisions or recommendations when
required?
No [ Points: +0 ]
23. Will there be a process in place to grant, monitor, and revoke access permission to the
system?
Yes [ Points: +1 ]
24. Will there be a mechanism to capture feedback by users of the system?
Yes [ Points: +1 ]
25. Will there be a recourse process planned or established for clients that wish to challenge the
decision?
No [ Points: +0 ]
26. Will the system enable human override of system decisions?
No [ Points: +0 ]
27. Will there be a process in place to log the instances when overrides were performed?
No [ Points: +0 ]
28. Will the audit trail include change control processes to record modifications to the system's
operation or performance?
No [ Points: +0 ]
29. Will you be preparing a concept case to the Government of Canada Enterprise Architecture
Review Board?
No [ Points: +0 ]
De-Risking and Mitigation Measures - Privacy
30. If your system uses or creates personal information, will you undertake or have you
undertaken a Privacy Impact Assessment, or updated an existing one?
No [ Points: +0 ]
31. Will you design and build security and privacy into your systems from the concept stage of
the project?
Yes [ Points: +1 ]
32. Will information be used within a closed system (i.e. no connections to the Internet, Intranet
or any other system)?
No [ Points: +0 ]
33. If the sharing of personal information is involved, has an agreement or arrangement with
appropriate safeguards been established?
No [ Points: +0 ]
34. Will you de-identify any personal information used or created by the system at any point in
the lifecycle?
No [ Points: +0 ]
